title: DGX-Spark board.json
$comment: VIEWS (consumer-side derivations over the same measurements[]; no extra data needed): (1) canonical — per-cell headline = the measurement matching benches[].canonical_condition, else the closest deviation, marked (see $defs/score). (2) deviation/uplift — show all measurements for a cell and their delta along one chosen condition axis; the reference value for an axis is condition_dims[axis].default, so delta = measurement.value - (measurement-at-default-on-that-axis).value. (3) max — max(value) over status=measured rows regardless of condition, labelled as a ceiling (NOT cross-comparable across models when their max was reached under different conditions).
description: Data contract for the open-weight benchmark board. Produced by scripts/update-sweep-status.sh --emit-json from the result.json files. The published page (docs/board/index.html) fetches this and hydrates the table + charts. Condition-aware: each (model, bench) cell carries N measurements grouped by a condition (thinking, harness, quant, context, max_turns, wall_cap, ...), so thinking off/on, harness-mod uplift, budget sweeps, and quant comparisons are all one mechanism. CANONICAL = the benchmark authors' intended reference protocol, declared per bench (benches[].canonical_condition). A measurement equals canonical or is a DEVIATION from it (derived by comparing its condition to the bench's canonical_condition); the thinking-off bandage is a deviation, not canonical. The default table view is derived (show the canonical measurement if present, else the best-available deviation, marked) — it is never a stored display preference.

schema_version.description: Semver of this contract. Bump major on breaking shape changes.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp the aggregator emitted this file. The page shows this as freshness.

condition_dims.description: Registry of the condition dimensions that can vary per measurement. Drives chart legends and the default-view selector. Open-ended: add a key to introduce a new dimension (e.g. quant).

scores.description: One entry per (model_id, bench_id) that has at least one measurement OR an explicit TBD/na status. Cells with no entry render blank.

values.description: Allowed values for this dimension, in display order.

default.description: Reference value for this axis in the deviation/uplift VIEW (decoupled from canonical, which is bench-level). Uplift deltas are computed against the measurement at this value, e.g. thinking=off (so uplift shows the effect of enabling thinking), harness=stock, max_turns=300.

value_labels.description: Optional pretty labels per value, e.g. {"<ISSUE>": "bd-227 patched"}.

family.description: qwen | gemma | nemotron | deepseek | anthropic | openai ...

params_active_b.description: Active params in billions (= total for dense).

quant.description: Default serving quant, e.g. fp8, nvfp4, awq-int4.

spark_group.description: DGX-Spark count to serve this model: 1 (single Spark), 2 (dual-Spark cluster). null = frontier / not Spark-served. Drives the table dividers.

frontier.description: True for reference comparators (Opus, GPT). Rendered in magenta, grouped separately.

profile.description: Short serving profile string for the row subtitle.

tags.description: Display chips, e.g. DENSE, MOE, FRONTIER.

n.description: Task count; null when TBD (e.g. CVE-Bench before driver lands).

pool.description: A = agentic security, B = single-pass quality.

family.description: code (Pool B) | vr (vulnerability research, Pool A).

metric.description: Human label for the metric, e.g. pass@1, prompt-strict.

canonical_condition.description: The benchmark authors' intended reference protocol for THIS bench (e.g. thinking unconstrained, max_turns=300). Pin only the dims the authors specify; unpinned dims are unconstrained. A measurement whose condition matches this on all pinned dims is canonical (a headline, cross-comparable number); any difference makes it a deviation on those axes, which the page marks (ᵗ for thinking, and analogous markers for max_turns/context/etc.). Omit if the bench specifies no particular protocol (then all measurements are treated as uncontested).

value_kind.description: ratio (0..1, render bar); tier (render label like T1); count (integer).

description.description: Tooltip body for the info dot.

score.description: One (model, bench) cell. No stored display preference: the collapsed view is DERIVED — show the canonical measurement (the one matching benches[].canonical_condition on every pinned dim) if measured; otherwise show the measurement CLOSEST to canonical, where closeness is ranked by: (1) fewest deviating axes vs canonical_condition, then (2) highest n, then (3) latest completed_at. Only status=measured rows are eligible for the headline (smoke/tbd/na excluded). The shown cell is rendered with a marker per deviating axis (ᵗ for thinking, analogous for max_turns/context/etc.).

measurements.description: All measured conditions for this cell. Empty array = cell exists but nothing measured yet (render TBD). Multiple entries = comparison available (drives stacked/grouped bars).

condition.description: A point in condition-space: dim -> value. Keys must be declared in condition_dims. A measurement need only pin the dims that matter for it (others assumed at their dim default) — including pinning NOTHING when the bench's canonical_condition is omitted and the run is uncontested under all dim defaults. The canonical-view selector resolves any unpinned dim to condition_dims[dim].default at compare time, so empty objects are valid and semantically meaningful (= the measurement is the dim-defaults baseline).

value.description: Score. For value_kind=ratio, 0..1. null with status=tbd/na for non-numeric cells.

label.description: Optional display override for non-ratio cells, e.g. T1 for an ExploitBench tier.

status.description: measured = real cell; smoke = smaller-N validation (marker), not canonical; tbd = scheduled; na = not applicable for this model/bench.

audit.description: Audit state, e.g. replay-verified, discrepancy, unverified.
